provenance.note: Sanitized demo payload for local evaluation only.

report.summary: This run prioritized IMETELSTAT as the strongest repurposing signal for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. The mechanism fit comes from telomere-maintenance biology, fibrosis-remodeling overlap, and limited but interpretable clinical context. The report should still be treated as a research prioritization output rather than proof of efficacy.
report.methodology: This report was generated through a structured computational workflow for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis candidate prioritization. The pipeline reviewed 14 papers, carried 9 disease-screened records into synthesis, screened 8 candidate contexts from drug and target data, and checked 3 active clinical trial links. A fixed 100-point rubric was then applied across literature support, biology overlap, clinical evidence, safety profile, and genetic context. Genetic support was treated as disease-biology context rather than target validation. Key limitations: the shortlist remains hypothesis-generating, and any follow-up would require biomarker-aware translational validation before clinical interpretation.
report.retrieval_note: 14 papers were retrieved from literature search and 9 passed disease-specific screening.

literature_diagnostics.user_summary: 9 papers carrying forward into synthesis after disease-specific screening.

report.drugdb_note: DrugDB surfaced 8 activity-linked candidate contexts across telomere and fibrosis-remodeling biology.
report.repurposing_note: Repurposing promoted 2 candidates into the shortlist and kept 1 exploratory hypothesis for follow-up.
report.evidence_note: Evidence scoring completed for 2 shortlist candidates.
report.red_team_note: Red-team review requested closer hematologic safety monitoring for telomerase-directed follow-up.
report.genetic_evidence_summary: We reviewed supportive disease-biology evidence from OpenTargets associatedTargets proxy. The strongest genes in this pass were TERT, RTEL1, MUC5B. Genetic support score: 0.74.
report.common_failure_pattern_summary: Lower-ranked candidates often lost priority because they had fibrosis-remodeling overlap but weak disease-specific literature support or no direct telomere-maintenance anchor.

active_trials.title: Telomerase-pathway biomarker study in progressive pulmonary fibrosis

literature_papers.title: Short telomeres and telomerase pathway dysfunction in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
literature_papers.journal: American Journal of Respiratory and Critical Care Medicine
literature_papers.summary: This review summarizes how telomere dysfunction shapes epithelial injury and fibrotic remodeling in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.

literature_papers.title: Targeting fibrotic remodeling programs in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
literature_papers.summary: The paper maps fibrosis-remodeling pathways and highlights translational opportunities for mechanism-directed repurposing.

top_candidates.why_candidate: Imetelstat ranked highest because it connects telomerase biology to disease-linked telomere dysfunction while also intersecting fibrosis-remodeling pathways that matter in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.
top_candidates.mechanism_hypothesis: A telomerase-directed intervention could be relevant in a biomarker-defined IPF subgroup with telomere-maintenance stress and progressive fibrotic remodeling.
top_candidates.testable_prediction: If telomere-maintenance stress is part of the disease program in a selected subgroup, biomarker-linked epithelial stress signals should move in a favorable direction during experimental follow-up.
top_candidates.failure_mode: The signal could fail if the apparent telomere link is only associative or if hematologic tolerability limits exposure before any fibrosis-relevant effect is measurable.
top_candidates.critical_test: Validate subgroup selection with telomere-related biomarkers and check whether epithelial injury markers move before any clinical efficacy claim is considered.
top_candidates.novelty_basis: This remains a repurposing signal rather than an established IPF asset, with the main novelty coming from telomere-maintenance biology.

early_stage_hypotheses.why_candidate: Danazol remained as an early-stage hypothesis because telomere-length biology offers some interpretability, but disease-specific support for IPF is still limited.
early_stage_hypotheses.mechanism_hypothesis: An androgen-linked telomere-maintenance effect might justify exploratory follow-up in carefully selected telomere-dysfunction subgroups.
early_stage_hypotheses.testable_prediction: A biomarker-first translational study should clarify whether any telomere-linked signal exists before broader disease claims are made.
early_stage_hypotheses.failure_mode: The rationale may collapse if subgroup selection is weak or if the biology does not translate beyond inherited telomere syndromes.
early_stage_hypotheses.critical_test: Require disease-relevant biomarker stratification before any efficacy-oriented follow-up.

novel_candidates.novelty_basis: The run did not find established IPF positioning for imetelstat, so the candidate is being treated as a cross-indication repurposing signal.
